message:
feat(install): Add version check for idempotent installation

Make install.sh fully idempotent by checking installed version before downloading:
- Add check_installed_version() function to compare installed vs target version
- Skip download and extraction if versions match (unless --force)
- Add --force flag to force reinstall even if same version
- Still run agent configuration (already idempotent) on skip

Resolves: git_safety_guard-b6vb.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.1","title":"[TUI] Add ratatui, comfy-table, indicatif dependencies","description":"Add the following dependencies to Cargo.toml:

```toml
[dependencies]
ratatui = { version = "0.30", default-features = false, features = ["crossterm"] }
comfy-table = "8.0"
indicatif = "0.17"
console = "0.15"
```

## Implementation Notes

- Use default-features = false for ratatui to minimize compile time
- crossterm feature provides cross-platform terminal support
- console crate provides TTY detection utilities

## Acceptance Criteria

- Dependencies compile without conflicts
- Existing tests still pass
- Cargo clippy passes
- No significant compile time regression (check with cargo build --timings)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:19.970925507-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:45:37.683696977-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:45:37.683696977-05:00","close_reason":"Added ratatui 0.30, comfy-table 7.2, indicatif 0.17, console 0.15.  {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.10","title":"[TUI] Colorblind-safe palette and high contrast mode","description":"# Task: Colorblind-Safe Palette and High Contrast Mode

## Background

Approximately 8% of men and 0.5% of women have some form of color vision deficiency. DCG should be usable by everyone, which means not relying solely on color to convey information.

## Implementation

### 1. Shape + Color Coding

Never use color alone. Combine with shapes/symbols:

```
✓ ALLOW   (green + checkmark)
⛔ BLOCK   (red + stop sign)
⚠️ WARNING (yellow + triangle)
ℹ️ INFO    (blue + circle)
```

### 2. Colorblind-Safe Palette

Use palette designed for common types:

| Semantic | Default | Protanopia | Deuteranopia | Tritanopia |
|----------|---------|------------|--------------|------------|
| Error    | #FF4444 | #0072B2    | #0072B2      | #D55E00    |
| Warning  | #FFAA00 | #E69F00    | #E69F00      | #CC79A7    |
| Success  | #44FF44 | #009E73    | #009E73      | #56B4E9    |
| Info     | #4444FF | #56B4E9    | #56B4E9      | #009E73    |

### 3. High Contrast Mode

```bash
# Enable via environment
export DCG_HIGH_CONTRAST=1

# Or via config
[output]
high_contrast = true
```

High contrast mode:
- Uses only black/white with bold for emphasis
- Increases border thickness
- Uses ASCII instead of Unicode box drawing

### 4. Theme Configuration

```toml
# ~/.config/dcg/config.toml
[theme]
palette = "default"  # default | colorblind | high-contrast
use_unicode = true   # false for ASCII-only
use_color = true     # false for monochrome
```

### 5. Automatic Detection

Respect standard environment variables:
- NO_COLOR: Disable all colors
- TERM=dumb: ASCII only, no colors
- COLORTERM: Detect 256/truecolor support

## Implementation in Theme System

```rust
pub fn get_palette() -> Palette {
    if env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
        return Palette::monochrome();
    }
    if env::var("DCG_HIGH_CONTRAST").is_ok() {
        return Palette::high_contrast();
    }
    match config.theme.palette.as_str() {
        "colorblind" => Palette::colorblind_safe(),
        "high-contrast" => Palette::high_contrast(),
        _ => Palette::default(),
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- All information conveyed by color also conveyed by shape/symbol
- Colorblind palette available and tested
- High contrast mode works
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- Documentation explains accessibility features
- Tested with color blindness simulator

## Testing

- Simulator testing with Sim Daltonism or Coblis
- Screen reader testing for prompt accessibility
- Verify all semantic info has non-color indicator","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.193228923-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.193228923-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.10","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T15:24:15.195029683-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","title":"[TUI] Create output module with theme system","description":"Create src/output/mod.rs as the root of the new output formatting module.

## Module Structure

```
src/output/
├── mod.rs         # Module exports and TTY detection
├── theme.rs       # Color scheme and border style definitions
├── denial.rs      # Denial message box renderer
├── tables.rs      # Table renderer using comfy-table
└── progress.rs    # Progress indicators using indicatif
```

## Theme System (theme.rs)

```rust
pub struct Theme {
    pub border_style: BorderStyle,
    pub severity_colors: SeverityColors,
    pub accent_color: Color,
}

pub struct SeverityColors {
    pub critical: Color,  // Red
    pub high: Color,      // Orange
    pub medium: Color,    // Yellow
    pub low: Color,       // Blue
}

impl Theme {
    pub fn default() -> Self { ... }
    pub fn no_color() -> Self { ... }  // Plain ASCII, no colors
}
```

## TTY Detection (mod.rs)

```rust
pub fn should_use_rich_output() -> bool {
    // 1. Check --json flag (explicit override)
    // 2. Check NO_COLOR env var
    // 3. Check if stdout is a TTY
    // 4. Check TERM env var for dumb terminals
    console::Term::stdout().is_term() 
        && std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_err()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Module compiles and exports theme types
- TTY detection works correctly
- NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- Theme system supports both rich and plain output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:21.115513884-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:48:42.219385729-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:48:42.219385729-05:00","close_reason":"Created src/output/{mod.rs,theme.rs} with TTY detection, Theme/SeverityColors/BorderStyle types, NO_COLOR support. 8 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:21.117473636-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:26.078684592-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","title":"[TUI] Implement denial message box renderer","description":"Create src/output/denial.rs with a rich denial message renderer.

## Design

The denial box should display:
1. Header with BLOCKED indicator and emoji
2. The command with span highlighting 
3. Pattern name and severity
4. Explanation (from Visual Explanations epic)
5. Safe alternatives as bullet list

## Implementation

```rust
pub struct DenialBox {
    command: String,
    span: HighlightSpan,
    pattern_id: String,
    severity: Severity,
    explanation: Option<String>,
    alternatives: Vec<String>,
}

impl DenialBox {
    pub fn render(&self, theme: &Theme) -> String {
        // Use ratatui Block widget for border
        // Use Paragraph widget for content
        // Fall back to ASCII box if no TTY
    }
    
    pub fn render_plain(&self) -> String {
        // Simple text format for non-TTY contexts
    }
}
```

## Border Style

```
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  ⛔  BLOCKED: Destructive Command Detected                    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Content here...                                              │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Integration Point

This integrates with src/hook.rs where denial messages are currently printed.

## Dependencies

- Requires output module (task 2) to be complete
- Works with existing highlight.rs span rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- Rich box renders correctly with Unicode borders
- Plain fallback works for non-TTY
- Explanation text wraps correctly at terminal width
- Works with existing span highlighting from highlight.rs","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:22.465040133-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:45:34.392211813-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:50:22.466546671-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:28.382198021-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","title":"[TUI] Implement scan results table renderer","description":"Create src/output/tables.rs with table rendering using comfy-table.

## Design

Tables should support:
1. Scan results (file, line, severity, pattern)
2. Stats output (rule, hits, allowed, noise%)
3. Pack list (id, name, pattern count)

## Implementation

```rust
use comfy_table::{Table, ContentArrangement, Row, Cell, Color, Attribute};

pub struct ScanResultsTable {
    results: Vec<ScanResult>,
}

impl ScanResultsTable {
    pub fn render(&self, theme: &Theme, max_width: usize) -> String {
        let mut table = Table::new();
        table
            .set_content_arrangement(ContentArrangement::Dynamic)
            .set_width(max_width)
            .set_header(vec!["File", "Line", "Severity", "Pattern"]);
            
        for result in &self.results {
            table.add_row(vec![
                Cell::new(&result.file).fg(Color::White),
                Cell::new(result.line).fg(Color::Cyan),
                severity_cell(&result.severity),
                Cell::new(&result.pattern_id).fg(Color::Yellow),
            ]);
        }
        
        table.to_string()
    }
}

fn severity_cell(severity: &Severity) -> Cell {
    match severity {
        Severity::Critical => Cell::new("CRIT").fg(Color::Red).add_attribute(Attribute::Bold),
        Severity::High => Cell::new("HIGH").fg(Color::DarkRed),
        Severity::Medium => Cell::new("MED").fg(Color::Yellow),
        Severity::Low => Cell::new("LOW").fg(Color::Blue),
    }
}
```

## Table Styles

Support multiple styles:
- Unicode box drawing (default for TTY)
- ASCII (for CI/CD environments)  
- Compact (minimal spacing)
- Markdown (for documentation output)

## Performance

comfy-table renders at ~30μs per table - well within budget.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Scan results render in clean table format
- Column widths adjust to terminal width
- Severity color coding works
- Plain ASCII fallback for non-TTY
- Markdown output option for docs","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.037171936-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.037171936-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:03.082807316-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:29.686472434-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","title":"[TUI] Implement progress indicators for scans","description":"Create src/output/progress.rs with progress bars and spinners using indicatif.

## Design

Progress indicators for:
1. File scanning (progress bar with file count)
2. Pattern loading (spinner during startup)
3. History analysis (progress for suggest-allowlist)

## Implementation

```rust
use indicatif::{ProgressBar, ProgressStyle, MultiProgress};

pub struct ScanProgress {
    bar: ProgressBar,
}

impl ScanProgress {
    pub fn new(total_files: u64) -> Self {
        let bar = ProgressBar::new(total_files);
        bar.set_style(
            ProgressStyle::default_bar()
                .template("{spinner:.green} [{bar:40.cyan/blue}] {pos}/{len} files ({eta})")
                .unwrap()
                .progress_chars("█▓░")
        );
        Self { bar }
    }
    
    pub fn tick(&self, file_path: &str) {
        self.bar.set_message(file_path.to_string());
        self.bar.inc(1);
    }
    
    pub fn finish_with_message(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.bar.finish_with_message(msg.to_string());
    }
}

pub fn spinner(message: &str) -> ProgressBar {
    let spinner = ProgressBar::new_spinner();
    spinner.set_style(
        ProgressStyle::default_spinner()
            .tick_strings(&["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"])
            .template("{spinner:.blue} {msg}")
            .unwrap()
    );
    spinner.set_message(message.to_string());
    spinner
}
```

## Integration Points

- src/scan.rs - Wrap file iteration with progress
- src/suggest.rs - Progress for history analysis
- src/main.rs - Spinner during config loading (if slow)

## Threshold for Progress

Only show progress bar when:
- Scanning >20 files (otherwise too fast to see)
- Operation takes >500ms

## Acceptance Criteria

- Progress bar shows during large scans
- Spinner shows during initialization
- ETA calculation is reasonably accurate
- Progress hidden in non-TTY contexts
- No visual artifacts when complete","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.138242251-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.138242251-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:21.161429022-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:30.956426596-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-advk.6","title":"[TUI] Integrate rich output into hook.rs","description":"Integrate the denial box renderer into src/hook.rs.

## Current State

hook.rs currently prints denial messages using basic println! with some ANSI color codes from the colored crate.

## Target State

Replace with rich denial box rendering:

```rust
use crate::output::{should_use_rich_output, DenialBox, Theme};

fn emit_denial(result: &EvaluationResult, command: &str) {
    let denial = DenialBox::new()
        .command(command)
        .span(&result.span)
        .pattern_id(&result.pattern_id)
        .severity(result.severity)
        .explanation(result.explanation.as_deref())
        .alternatives(&result.alternatives);
        
    if should_use_rich_output() {
        eprintln!("{}", denial.render(&Theme::default()));
    } else {
        eprintln!("{}", denial.render_plain());
    }
}
```

## Backward Compatibility

- Ensure JSON output mode still works
- Ensure non-TTY contexts get plain text
- Ensure tests that check stderr output still pass

## Migration Path

1. Add new rendering path behind feature flag first
2. Test thoroughly
3. Make default
4. Remove old code path

## Acceptance Criteria

- Denial messages render in rich box format
- JSON mode still returns machine-readable output
- Existing tests pass with minor updates
- Performance within budget (<1ms overhead)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:49.84561366-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:49.84561366-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:51:49.873465188-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-advk.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T13:53:32.08939333-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

@@ -39,6 +39,7 @@ LOCK_FILE="/tmp/dcg-install.lock"
 SYSTEM=0
 NO_GUM=0
 NO_CHECKSUM=0
+FORCE_INSTALL=0
 
 # Detect gum for fancy output (https://github.com/charmbracelet/gum)
 HAS_GUM=0
@@ -288,6 +289,32 @@ is_agent_detected() {
   return 1
 }
 
+# Check if installed version matches target
+# Returns 0 if versions match, 1 if they differ or dcg not installed
+check_installed_version() {
+  local target_version="$1"
+  if [ ! -x "$DEST/dcg" ]; then
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  local installed_version
+  installed_version=$("$DEST/dcg" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/.*\([0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\).*/\1/')
+
+  if [ -z "$installed_version" ]; then
+    return 1
+  fi
+
+  # Normalize versions (strip 'v' prefix)
+  local target_clean="${target_version#v}"
+  local installed_clean="${installed_version#v}"
+
+  if [ "$target_clean" = "$installed_clean" ]; then
+    return 0
+  fi
+
+  return 1
+}
+
 resolve_version() {
   if [ -n "$VERSION" ]; then return 0; fi
 
@@ -404,7 +431,7 @@ usage() {
   cat <<EOFU
 Usage: install.sh [--version vX.Y.Z] [--dest DIR] [--system] [--easy-mode] [--verify] \\
                   [--artifact-url URL] [--checksum HEX] [--checksum-url URL] [--quiet] \\
-                  [--no-gum] [--no-verify]
+                  [--no-gum] [--no-verify] [--force]
 
 Options:
   --version vX.Y.Z   Install specific version (default: latest)
@@ -416,6 +443,7 @@ Options:
   --quiet            Suppress non-error output
   --no-gum           Disable gum formatting even if available
   --no-verify        Skip checksum verification (for testing only)
+  --force            Force reinstall even if same version is installed
 EOFU
 }
 
@@ -433,6 +461,7 @@ while [ $# -gt 0 ]; do
     --quiet|-q) QUIET=1; shift;;
     --no-gum) NO_GUM=1; shift;;
     --no-verify) NO_CHECKSUM=1; shift;;
+    --force) FORCE_INSTALL=1; shift;;
     -h|--help) usage; exit 0;;
     *) shift;;
   esac
@@ -464,6 +493,38 @@ fi
 
 resolve_version
 
+# Check if already at target version (skip download if so, unless --force)
+if [ "$FORCE_INSTALL" -eq 0 ] && check_installed_version "$VERSION"; then
+  ok "dcg $VERSION is already installed at $DEST/dcg"
+  info "Use --force to reinstall"
+
+  # Still run agent configuration (idempotent) to ensure hooks are set up
+  detect_predecessor
+  if [ "$PREDECESSOR_FOUND" -eq 1 ]; then
+    show_upgrade_banner
+  fi
+
+  # Configure agents (these are already idempotent)
+  configure_claude_code "$CLAUDE_SETTINGS" "0"
+  configure_gemini "$GEMINI_SETTINGS"
+
+  # Show final summary even when skipping download
+  echo ""
+  case "$CLAUDE_STATUS" in
+    already) ok "Claude Code: Already configured" ;;
+    merged|created) ok "Claude Code: Configured" ;;
+    *) : ;;
+  esac
+  case "$GEMINI_STATUS" in
+    already) ok "Gemini CLI: Already configured" ;;
+    merged|created) ok "Gemini CLI: Configured" ;;
+    skipped|"") info "Gemini CLI: Not installed (skipped)" ;;
+    *) : ;;
+  esac
+
+  exit 0
+fi
+
 mkdir -p "$DEST"
 OS=$(uname -s | tr 'A-Z' 'a-z')
 ARCH=$(uname -m)
